query: How many consecutive calendar days of PTO require special permission from the manager?
ground_truth: For time off exceeding 25 consecutive calendar days (including weekends and holidays), special permission is required from your Manager, People Business Partner, and the Absence Management Team.

query: Can GitLab team members substitute PTO for sick time?
ground_truth: No. Team members are not allowed to substitute PTO for sick time. If taking time off for medical needs or sick time, they must follow the Sick Time Policy.

query: What is the minimum total PTO GitLab strongly recommends taking each year?
ground_truth: GitLab strongly recommends taking at least two consecutive weeks of time off and at minimum 25 days total throughout the year.

query: Who should a GitLab team member report harassment to?
ground_truth: Team members should report harassment directly to the Chief People Officer, Team Member Relations, or a People Business Partner immediately for thorough investigation.

query: Does GitLab's anti-harassment policy apply only to internal team interactions?
ground_truth: No. The Anti-harassment policy applies to GitLab team members' interactions with customers, vendors, and community members, not just internal interactions.

query: How many days before the hire date is the onboarding issue opened at GitLab?
ground_truth: The People Operations Specialist opens the onboarding issue at least 4 days prior to the hire date.

query: How long is the due date for the main section of the GitLab onboarding issue?
ground_truth: The main section of the onboarding issue has a due date of 30 days.

query: What is the effective date of GitLab's Anti-Retaliation Policy?
ground_truth: The Anti-Retaliation Policy has an effective date of 21 April 2023.

query: What is the focus of GitLab's engineering direction in terms of product quality?
ground_truth: GitLab's engineering focus is to make GitLab enterprise grade in all its abilities and to support the AI efforts required to successfully launch AI features to General Availability.

query: What infrastructure work supports GitLab's disaster recovery and availability goals?
ground_truth: Teams are collaborating on improving disaster recovery and support offerings through ongoing work with GitLab Dedicated and Cells infrastructure, with the goal of improved availability and service recovery.

query: What are the three components of career development for engineers at GitLab?
ground_truth: The three important components of developing one's career at GitLab are Structure, Growth, and something to be discussed with the manager â€” specifically the career coaching conversation, promotion track, and individual development.

query: What determines the engineering How and When in product development at GitLab?
ground_truth: Engineering determines the How (technical implementation) and When (scheduling) of platform releases, while Product Management looks ahead for the What and Why.

query: What guides engineering culture at GitLab?
ground_truth: Engineering culture at GitLab encompasses the processes, workflows, principles and priorities that all stem from GitLab Values.

query: What percentage of engineering time does Product Management prioritize at GitLab?
ground_truth: Product Management prioritizes 60% of engineering time, and Engineering prioritizes 40% of time on initiatives that improve the product, underlying platform, and foundational technologies.

query: What does CREDIT stand for in GitLab's values?
ground_truth: GitLab's six core values are Collaboration, Results for Customers, Efficiency, Diversity Inclusion and Belonging, Iteration, and Transparency, and together they spell CREDIT.

query: Why did GitLab condense its values into the CREDIT acronym?
ground_truth: GitLab had more values previously but it was difficult to remember them all. They condensed them, created the CREDIT acronym, and listed operating principles to guide behavior.

query: Are GitLab's values a fixed document or do they change over time?
ground_truth: GitLab values are a living document. They have been documented, refined, and revised based on lessons learned in the course of doing business.

query: In how many countries does GitLab operate as a remote company?
ground_truth: GitLab operates in 65+ countries as an all-remote, asynchronous company.

query: Who can suggest improvements to GitLab's values?
ground_truth: Everyone is welcome to suggest improvements to GitLab's values. MRs to update the values should be assigned to the Chief People Officer.

query: What is GitLab's approach to remote work described as?
ground_truth: GitLab describes itself as all-remote since inception, operating as an asynchronous, no-office company consistently recognized as a great place to work.
